title: Outbound Trap Sending
text:
[REQ-0034] The library MUST expose a public function on `Agent` for sending SNMPv3 Trap PDUs (SNMPv2-Trap-PDU as defined in RFC 3416 §4.2.6).
[REQ-0035] The function MUST block until delivery to all destinations has been attempted and MUST return per-destination results.

[REQ-0036] The agent MUST send traps over plain UDP with no encryption or authentication.

[REQ-0037] The agent MUST record the time of `Agent` construction and use it as the basis for generating `sysUpTime.0` values.
[REQ-0038] The agent MUST automatically populate `sysUpTime.0` as the first varbind of every outbound Trap PDU.
[REQ-0039] The `TrapPdu` type MUST NOT include a field for `sysUpTime.0`.

[REQ-0040] The function MUST accept a `TrapPdu` value containing the trap OID (`snmpTrapOID.0`) and a list of additional varbinds representing the trap payload.
[REQ-0041] The agent MUST prepend `sysUpTime.0` and `snmpTrapOID.0` as the first two varbinds in the outbound PDU as required by RFC 3416 §4.2.6.

[REQ-0042] The function MUST accept a list of destination socket addresses.
[REQ-0043] The function MUST return an error immediately if the destination list is empty, without sending any PDU.

[REQ-0044] The agent MUST send the trap to every destination in the list.
[REQ-0045] Failure to deliver to one destination MUST NOT prevent delivery attempts to the remaining destinations.

[REQ-0046] The agent MUST NOT send SNMP Inform PDUs; only unacknowledged Trap PDUs are supported.

[REQ-0047] The function MUST return a result indicating per-destination success or failure so that the caller can log or act on individual delivery errors.

**Rationale:**
Having the library generate `sysUpTime.0` from its own construction time removes a burden from the caller and ensures the value is consistent and accurate.
RFC 3416 §4.2.6 mandates `sysUpTime.0` and `snmpTrapOID.0` as the first two varbinds; enforcing this in the library prevents malformed traps.
A blocking call with per-destination results gives the caller a simple synchronous API while the actual sending is handled by the event loop via the command channel.
Plain UDP keeps trap delivery simple; traps are unacknowledged notifications and do not require the overhead of TLS.
